channel.title: AI & Tech News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregated AI and Tech news from 11 sources

item.title: Exclusive-Nvidia preparing Groq chips that can be sold in Chinese market, sources say
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Commentary: In the shadow of Hormuz, Trump is still pursuing his tariff agenda
item.description: [CNA Tech] The latest US trade probes are designed for a specific political purpose - to reclaim lost leverage following the striking down of the “Liberation Day” tariffs, says Kevin Chen from the S Rajaratnam School of International Studies.

item.title: Mistral bets on ‘build-your-own AI’ as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Mistral Forge lets enterprises train custom AI models from scratch on their own data, challenging rivals that rely on fine-tuning and retrieval-based approaches.

item.title: S’pore gold sellers bring in more stock, build vaults to meet higher demand amid Middle East crisis
item.description: [Straits Times] Besides middle-aged buyers, young consumers are also snapping up items like gold bars and coins in recent weeks.

item.title: Why Garry Tan’s Claude Code setup has gotten so much love, and hate
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Thousands of people are trying Garry Tan's Claude Code setup, which was shared on GitHub. And everyone has an opinion: even Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini.

item.title: US securities regulator issues long-awaited crypto guidance
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Nvidia sales opportunity for Blackwell, Rubin chips more than $1 trillion by 2027
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Nvidia sales opportunity for Blackwell, Rubin chips more than $1 trillion by 2027
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Exclusive-Amazon CEO sees AI doubling prior AWS sales projections to $600 billion by 2036
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: The Pentagon is developing alternatives to Anthropic, report says
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] After their dramatic falling-out, it doesn't seem as though Anthropic and the Pentagon are getting back together.

item.title: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 is like motion smoothing for video games, but worse
item.description: [The Verge AI] Yesterday Nvidia revealed its latest upscaling tech, called DLSS 5, which it described as "the company's most significant breakthrough in computer graphics since the debut of real-time ray tracing in 2018." Sounds good, until you actually see it. According to Nvidia, the tech "infuses pixels with ph

item.title: BuzzFeed debuts AI slop apps in bid for new revenue
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] BuzzFeed unveiled new AI-powered social apps at SXSW, but its demos drew muted reactions.

item.title: Now everyone in the US is getting Google’s personalized Gemini AI
item.description: [The Verge AI] Google announced on Tuesday that all users in the US will now have access to its Personal Intelligence feature, which lets you connect various Google apps to provide context for Gemini's responses and suggestions. Access was previously limited to Google AI Pro and AI Ultra subscribers. Now, free-tie

item.title: Google’s Personal Intelligence feature is expanding to all US users
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Personal Intelligence allows Google's AI assistant to tap into your Google ecosystem, such as Gmail and Google Photos, to provide more tailored responses.

item.title: OpenAI expands government footprint with AWS deal, report says
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] OpenAI has reportedly signed a partnership with AWS to sell its AI systems to the U.S. government for classified and unclassified work, marking an expansion beyond its Pentagon deal last month.

item.title: Microsoft appoints a new Copilot boss after AI leadership shake-up
item.description: [The Verge AI] Microsoft is doing another executive shuffle today to reorganize how it engineers its Copilot assistant. Different teams have been working on the consumer and commercial sides of Copilot for years, but Microsoft is about to unify parts of them in an effort to create a more cohesive Copilot for busin

item.title: The future of code is exciting and terrifying
item.description: [The Verge AI] Suddenly it seems like everyone's a coder. Or, at the very least, like they play one in the Claude Code app. But even for the seasoned pros, the act of software development is changing fast - many people are writing less code themselves and instead spending their time managing agents and projects. S

item.title: 科氪｜不止平整，更懂高效！全球最平整折叠手机 OPPO Find N6 全球发布
item.description:
[36氪] 2026 年 3 月 17 日，中国·东莞 —— 今日，折叠旗舰 OPPO Find N6 在 OPPO 滨海湾园区面向全球正式发布。在这座为工程师打造的创新乐园里，OPPO 以 8 年深耕折叠领域的技术沉淀，通过新一代钛合金天穹铰链与天穹记忆玻璃两大颠覆性黑科技，彻底破解困扰折叠屏行业 8 年的折痕难题，让 Find N6 成为德国莱茵 TÜV 实验室测试过的「全球最平整折叠手机」，引领折叠屏正式迈入「无感折痕，久用平整」的全新世代；配以极致的工业设计、无短板的旗舰硬件、重构效率的生产力AI外挂等全面旗舰体验，OPPO Find N6 重新定义折叠旗舰新标杆。
官方图片 OPP

item.title: “纸带龙头”收购国防科大系硬科技：洁美科技的豪赌为何难打动市场
item.description: [钛媒体] “材料商”能否真正玩转“高端装备”？

item.title: Founder of Chinese Internet Security Software Firm Zhou Explains How OpenClaw Kills Software
item.description: [钛媒体] Why did platforms funded lavishly by the big-tech giants over the past year failed to gain traction, while an open-source OpenClaw developed by one person managed to dominate the industry?

item.title: Nvidia faces gamer backlash over 'breakthrough' AI graphics feature
item.description: [BBC Technology] Nvidia says the tool will transform game graphics - critics warn it could squeeze out artistic expression.

item.title: Middle managers are the missing link in AI adoption
item.description: [MarTech] Executives set the vision and teams test tools, but transformation happens in the middle. Equip managers to drive AI-powered workflow change. The post Middle managers are the missing link in AI adoption appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: 桌游DM、儿童陪护……在清华，我们看到了龙虾九种「叛逆」的玩法
item.description: [36氪] 文｜王欣逸 钟楚笛 编辑｜周鑫雨 3月15日，清华校园。看惯了市面上一水儿的提效工具、高深莫测的部署教程，在由清北学生社区AttraX发起的“Outlier” OpenClaw黑客松路演上，我们看到了一些跳脱的、叛逆的“龙虾”玩法。 OpenClaw，俗称龙虾，当下最火的Agent开源框架。它能在计算机本地及云端部署，全面接管用户电脑，自动完成文件处理、邮件收发等任务。 随着2026年3月，腾讯等大厂接连举办免费的龙虾部署活动，这项原本局限在极客圈子里的狂欢，逐渐蔓延至大众。 龙虾的火热，超乎预期。最初，AttraX团队把活动定位为“AI浪潮下的跨学科碰撞”。但活动报名人数

item.title: 终极ＡＩ是否会拿走人类所有权限？
item.description: [钛媒体] AI的发展本质上是一部人类权限的让渡史，从生存劳作到认知决策，从创造审美到治理司法，乃至最终的定义意义权，AI正在逐层接管。然而，当AI拿走所有功能性权限后，人类将保留最后的“根权限”——定义目标的自由意志、体验的绝对主权以及赋予世界意义的能力。这并非人类的终结，而是从“劳动者”向“意义赋予者”的解放。

item.title: How Invisalign Became the World’s Biggest User of 3D Printers
item.description: [WIRED AI] Joe Hogan, Align Technology’s plastics-nerd CEO, says you shouldn’t eat with your aligners and that you don’t need to wear your retainers every night.

item.title: 从DeepSeek冲击到180亿美元估值，月之暗面如何实现绝境反弹？
item.description: [钛媒体] 3个月估值翻4倍，月之暗面凭什么值180亿美元？

item.title: AI硬件IPO潮继续席卷：四家公司扎堆港股，硬科技融资窗口继续开闸
item.description: [钛媒体] 卖铲人的胜利：AI军备竞赛下的设备与制造重构。

item.title: 36氪晚报｜苹果首席运营官Sabih Khan现身深圳；马斯克：电动超跑Roadster或将于四月下旬亮相；华为面向AI推理场景发布新一代AI数据基础设施
item.description: [36氪] 大公司： 西门子与Rittal达成数据中心能源基础设施战略合作伙伴关系 西门子和Rittal已建立战略合作伙伴关系，共同开发面向未来的产品，为数据中心更高效的电力分发提供可持续解决方案。（财联社） 腾讯音乐：2025年全年总收入329亿元，同比增长15.8% 36氪获悉，腾讯音乐发布截至2025年12月31日止第四季度及全年的未经审计财务业绩。腾讯音乐第四季度总收入86.4亿元，同比增长15.9%，调整后净利润25.8亿元，同比增长7.6%；全年总收入329亿元，同比增长15.8%，调整后净利润99.2亿元，同比增长22.0%。全年在线音乐订阅收入同比增长16.0%至176.6

item.title: 阿里抛出“悟空”，B端AI之争告别“养龙虾”竞赛
item.description: [钛媒体] 国内企业级AI Agent的竞争，已从自动化功能的接入与迭代，正式进入AI原生工作平台生态比拼。

item.title: ComfortDelGro to raise taxi fares, add app fee as Iran war drives up fuel costs
item.description: [Straits Times] The higher charges will be in place from March 24 to May 31.

item.title: Sears Exposed AI Chatbot Phone Calls and Text Chats to Anyone on the Web
item.description: [WIRED AI] Customer conversations with chatbots can include contact information and personal details that make it easier for scammers to launch phishing attacks and commit fraud.

item.title: 拿下半导体领域关键客户粉体材料采购订单，「矽瓷新能」完成数千万元A轮融资 | 36氪首发
item.description: [36氪] 文 | 张冰冰 编辑 | 阿至 36氪获悉，北京矽瓷新能科技有限公司（以下简称「矽瓷新能」）近期完成数千万元A轮融资，本轮融资由中关村资本领投，北洋海棠基金跟投，老股东金桥基金、尚势资本持续加码。资金将重点用于7N级高纯合成石英砂产能扩建、3C-碳化硅粉体量产线建设，加速推进半导体核心材料国产化替代。 「矽瓷新能」是36氪长期关注的企业，聚焦半导体上游关键材料研发与生产，布局了合成石英砂、氮化铝、碳化硅等产品，覆盖刻蚀环、光掩膜版等半导体核心工艺环节结构件，以及单晶衬底、芯片热管理等应用场景。 2025年11月，「矽瓷新能」通过了关键客户

item.title: GDC上，中国游戏大厂秀了秀「AI肌肉」
item.description: [36氪] 文丨贝果树 编辑丨刘士武 “行业处于变动之中，我们也在随之改变”是本届GDC的主题——也因此，今年大会的名字从“游戏开发者大会”更名为了“GDC游戏节”。 近几年来，随着中国游戏厂商技术和产品力的提升，越来越多的国内游戏公司开始成为GDC的主角。而近年来的AI浪潮，更是让很多厂商有了弯道超车的机会。 2026年3月9日-13日期间，第40届GDC于美国旧金山举办。作为全球规模最大的游戏开发者会议，本届GDC吸引了超3万名参会者、超400家参展商，举办了700余场会议与活动。 AI成为了本次GDC的主线——今年，Al议题数量达105场，增长超110%。 主题的更新与行业发

item.title: 马斯克用AI卖货，这家中国公司用AI管理18万个货架：无人零售进入“智能体时代”
item.description: [36氪] 2026年，AI行业正站在一个历史性的分水岭上。 不久前，谷歌在其《AI智能体趋势2026》报告中预言，人工智能正经历从“只会回答问题的工具”向能自主拆解任务、跨系统干活的“得力助手”的关键跃迁 。 而在这一轮全球AI浪潮中，一个极具象征意义的细节来自埃隆·马斯克：在2025年4月的Grok-4发布会及后续的Vending Bench测试中，马斯克用事实证明了AI不仅能聊天，更能卖货：马斯克以Vending-Bench 自动售货机模拟运营测试为例，让 AI 完整管理一台自动贩卖机，包括选品、定价、补货、供应商谈判、现金流管理，展示 Grok 4 的长周期商业决策与持续盈利能力

item.title: Why petrol prices go up like rockets but fall like feathers – in S’pore and globally
item.description: [Straits Times] Pump prices of petrol and diesel in Singapore recently crossed highs set during the Ukraine crisis in 2022.

item.title: Middle East oil becomes most expensive in the world as Iran war cuts supply
item.description: [Straits Times] War-driven supply shock sends Middle East crude benchmarks to records, straining Asian refiners.

item.title: Emirates flying near-empty planes back to Dubai as locals depart
item.description: [Straits Times] State-owned carrier has seen its operations severely disrupted by the threats from Iranian missiles in its airspace.

item.title: SK Group chairman says chip wafer shortage to last until 2030, trying to stabilise memory prices
item.description: [Straits Times] SK Hynix's CEO may unveil plans to stabilise DRAM chip prices.

item.title: AI firm Anthropic seeks weapons expert to stop users from 'misuse'
item.description: [BBC Technology] The artificial intelligence firm says it wants to prevent "catastrophic misuse" of its systems.

item.title: The latest AI-powered martech news and releases
item.description: [MarTech] Zendesk is betting AI agents will handle most customer service interactions, and its $200M-plus Forethought acquisition shows how serious it is. The post The latest AI-powered martech news and releases appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: AI is repricing the marketing stack, not collapsing it
item.description: [MarTech] AI is making coordination tools easier to replicate, putting pricing pressure on parts of the marketing stack that deliver convenience rather than operational risk. The post AI is repricing the marketing stack, not collapsing it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: China AI startup Moonshot snags funds at US$18 billion valuation
item.description: [Business Times] [BEIJING] Moonshot AI is seeking to raise as much as US$1 billion in an expanded funding round that would value the startup...

item.title: US Commerce Department withdraws planned rule on AI chip exports
item.description: [Business Times] [NEW YORK] The US Commerce Department withdrew a planned rule on artificial intelligence (AI) chip exports on Friday (Mar 13), the latest...

item.title: The price isn’t right: Oil spike fuels risks in South-east Asia’s tech scene
item.description: [Business Times] RISING oil prices triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East could quickly drive up operating costs within South-east Asia’s tech...

item.title: She spent 16 hours on Instagram in a day. It's up to a jury to decide if Meta is to blame
item.description: [BBC Technology] A landmark lawsuit will set the stage for thousands of people who say social media platforms are intentionally addictive.

item.title: DLSS 5 looks like a real-time generative AI filter for video games
item.description: [The Verge AI] Nvidia announced DLSS 5 on Monday during its GTC conference, and based on early reactions, it's going to be a divisive update, with some reactions calling it "slop" that unacceptably alters artistic intent. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is calling this the "GPT moment for graphics - blending hand-crafted

item.title: Teens sue Elon Musk’s xAI over Grok’s AI-generated CSAM
item.description: [The Verge AI] Three Tennessee teens are suing Elon Musk's xAI over claims that the company's Grok AI chatbot generated sexualized images and videos of themselves as minors, as reported earlier by The Washington Post. The proposed class action lawsuit, filed on Monday, accuses Musk and other xAI leaders of knowing

item.title: Teens sue Musk's xAI over Grok's pornographic images of them
item.description: [BBC Technology] Musk's AI chatbot has created millions of fake sexualised images, experts say.

item.title: Salesforce Agentforce: What you need to know
item.description: [MarTech] We've updated our page on Agentforce with details of the Agentforce Contact Center release. The post Salesforce Agentforce: What you need to know appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: The new must-haves in B2B ecommerce tech stacks go beyond CRM and CMS
item.description: [MarTech] As B2B buyers expect more B2C-like experiences, ecommerce marketers need tools that go far beyond the basics. The post The new must-haves in B2B ecommerce tech stacks go beyond CRM and CMS appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Firms urged to check if other users edited their data on Companies House
item.description: [BBC Technology] The glitch meant logged-in users could view and edit other companies' details without their consent.

item.title: Models Are Applying to Be the Face of AI Scams
item.description: [WIRED AI] Dozens of Telegram channels reviewed by WIRED include job listings for “AI face models.” The (mostly) women who land these gigs are likely being used to dupe victims out of their money.

item.title: Is this product 'human-made'? The race to establish an AI-free logo
item.description: [BBC Technology] The backlash to the growing use of the tech has led to an explosion in attempts to come up with 'AI-Free' logo that could be used globally.

item.title: Most consumers use AI, but few fully trust it
item.description: [MarTech] New research shows AI is already shaping product discovery and purchases, but a large gap between usage and trust is creating new challenges for marketers. The post Most consumers use AI, but few fully trust it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Google's AI Search Results Love to Refer You Back to Google
item.description: [WIRED AI] The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.

item.title: China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
item.description: [WIRED AI] Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.

item.title: Gamers’ Worst Nightmares About AI Are Coming True
item.description: [WIRED AI] From the global RAM shortage driving up console prices to job loss in the industry, gaming is shaping up to be one of the AI boom's biggest casualties.
